summary: Gruber promotes an Amazon pre-order deal for Mac OS X 10.4 Tiger that includes a $35 rebate, bringing the price to $95. He has placed an affiliate link in the site's banner, framing it as a way for readers to save money while supporting Daring Fireball. He estimates around 90 percent of his readership plans to upgrade. The offer is US-only, with no availability yet on Amazon's Canadian or UK stores.
key_points: Amazon offers Mac OS X Tiger for pre-order with a $35 rebate, bringing the cost to $95
Gruber uses affiliate links as a revenue model for Daring Fireball, being transparent about it
He assumes the vast majority (90%) of his audience will upgrade to Tiger, reflecting the enthusiasm of the Mac community
money_quotes: If you're planning on upgrading to Tiger (and I'm guessing somewhere around 90 percent of you are), this is a good way to save yourself $35 and support Daring Fireball at the same time.
key_insight: Gruber leverages his audience's near-universal enthusiasm for Mac OS upgrades to transparently promote an affiliate deal that benefits both readers and his site.
